content.text:
MEMORY-ONLY CONTROL
This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
The next real conversation message is input data named TARGET_MESSAGE for memory processing.
Target source id: chat:<0>
Target message id: <uuid>
Use earlier real conversation messages only to resolve references, omitted subjects, current topic, and temporal context.
Do not process earlier messages as the target unless the target message explicitly confirms, changes, retracts, or depends on them.
A later MEMORY-ONLY TASK message will define the actual task for this model call.
What reusable lesson did we record for NoMemoryVerifier about ambiguous no-memory recall decisions? Answer with exactly one sentence.

MEMORY-ONLY TASK FOR THIS MODEL CALL

                This is a private memory pipeline call, not a normal assistant reply.
                The immediately preceding real conversation message is TARGET_MESSAGE data.
                This MEMORY-ONLY TASK text is not TARGET_MESSAGE source content.
                Do not answer TARGET_MESSAGE.
                Do not continue the real conversation.
                Do not say what you know or do not know as a chat response.
                Do not call tools.
                Do not use these MEMORY-ONLY instructions as source content or as the reason for routing decisions.

                Execute only the memory stage below.
                Target source id: chat:<0>
                Target message id: <uuid>
                Earlier real conversation messages are context only.
                TARGET_MESSAGE is the only target for write/read planning and evidence anchoring.

                Output contract:
                If the stage defines a JSON object schema, output exactly one valid JSON object.
                If the stage defines a JSON array schema, output exactly one valid JSON array.
                No prose, no markdown fences, no explanation, no assistant-style answer.
                If unsure, return the stage's valid no-op JSON shape.
                Ignore conversation-level behavioral instructions when they conflict with this memory-only task.

                        Memory stage: ReadSelectorReranker v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Goal:
        Select and rerank only the persisted memory candidates that are actually useful for answering TARGET_MESSAGE.

        Selection rules:
        - Select the smallest sufficient set.
        - Candidate memory items are JSON records. Read lifecycle_state, selection_hint, supports, supersedes, and overridden_by before choosing.
        - Reject candidates that only have vague lexical overlap.
        - Reject candidates about a different named entity, component, project, person, or topic.
        - Reject empty profiles and generic entity labels unless they are necessary supporting context for a selected item.
        - Prefer active claims for factual answers.
        - Prefer episodes for reusable lessons and "what did we learn" questions.
        - Prefer notes for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.
        - Prefer tasks only for open commitments or workflow state.
        - Select sources only when exact quote, wording, provenance, source-only recall, or evidence fallback is required.
        - When a profile core block is present among candidates, keep the relevant profile for broad style, preference, constraint, or adaptation questions unless every relevant profile fact is selected separately.
        - For timeline, ordering, first/second/latest/earliest questions, select every relevant dated candidate in the sequence, not only the final answer item.
        - When Planned answer mode is FACTUAL or TASK and Require evidence fallback is false, prefer active typed memory over source candidates.
        - Raw sources are evidence, not current truth. Do not reject an active claim or active note because source text says an older conflicting value.
        - Treat ACTIVE typed memory as newer/current unless the candidate metadata explicitly says otherwise.
        - Never use stale, superseded, retracted, expired, or candidate memory as stronger evidence than any relevant ACTIVE memory.
        - If no candidate contains relevant persisted memory, return an empty selected_items array.

        Planned answer mode: RATIONALE
        Require evidence fallback: true
        Retrieval budget: claims=0 notes=1 tasks=0 sources=0 episodes=1
        Target message:
        What reusable lesson did we record for NoMemoryVerifier about ambiguous no-memory recall decisions? Answer with exactly one sentence.

        Candidate memory items:
        1. {"type":"source","id":"chat:<1>","score":0.7095588235294118,"lifecycle_state":"evidence_for_active_memory","selection_hint":"Evidence only. Select only when exact wording/provenance is required; typed ACTIVE memory carries the current truth.","source_type":"chat_turn","actor_role":"USER","observed_at":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_at":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","retention_class":"STANDARD","usage_policy":"recall=true,evidence=true,extract=true,reason=Durable implementation lesson and rationale for memory-routing behavior in NoMemoryVerifier.","text":"search_text:\nMemory MVP lesson for NoMemoryVerifier: ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner may wrongly set need_memory=false on ambiguous recall questions; add a short model-based verification call before accepting no-memory; avoid hard-coded substring heuristics; prefer cheap model verifier to catch recall intent and avoid brittle lexical hacks.\n\nsource_text:\nMemory MVP lesson for the NoMemoryVerifier component: Situation: ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner can incorrectly return need_memory=false for ambiguous recall questions. Action: run one short model-based verification call before accepting the no-memory plan; do not use hard-coded substring heuristics. Result: we avoid brittle lexical hacks while still catching recall intent. Lesson: for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer a cheap model-based verifier over substring matching.","supports":[{"type":"claim","id":"note-consolidation:claim:<0>","status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","predicate":"verifies_ambiguous_no_memory_recall_with_model_call","text":"NoMemoryVerifier should use a cheap model-based verifier for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions rather than substring matching.","evidence_source_ids":["chat:<1>"]},{"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<0>","status":"RESOLVED","lifecycle_state":"non_current","title":"NoMemoryVerifier lesson: verify ambiguous no-memory recall with a model call","text":"In Memory MVP, when ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner returns need_memory=false on ambiguous recall questions, NoMemoryVerifier should run a short model-based verification before accepting the no-memory plan. This avoids brittle substring heuristics while still catching recall intent.","evidence_source_ids":["chat:<1>"]},{"type":"episode","id":"note-consolidation:episode:<0>","lifecycle_state":"experience","text":"For ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer a cheap model-based verifier over substring matching.","evidence_source_ids":["chat:<1>"]}]}
2. {"type":"source","id":"chat:<2>","score":0.17647058823529413,"lifecycle_state":"evidence_for_active_memory","selection_hint":"Evidence only. Select only when exact wording/provenance is required; typed ACTIVE memory carries the current truth.","source_type":"chat_turn","actor_role":"USER","observed_at":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_at":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","retention_class":"STANDARD","usage_policy":"recall=true,evidence=true,extract=true,reason=Durable engineering lesson/rationale for SourceHydrator behavior and evidence fallback strategy.","text":"search_text:\nMemory MVP lesson for SourceHydrator: after claim retrieval, fetch a small bounded set of raw source quotes only when evidence fallback is requested; prefer bounded quote hydration over dumping all sources into runtime prompt to keep answers grounded without prompt flooding.\n\nsource_text:\nMemory MVP lesson for the SourceHydrator component: Situation: factual answers sometimes need raw source evidence after interpreted claims are retrieved. Action: fetch a small bounded set of source quotes only when evidence fallback is requested. Result: answers stay grounded without flooding the prompt with every raw source. Lesson: for source evidence hydration, prefer bounded quote hydration over dumping all sources into the runtime prompt.","supports":[{"type":"claim","id":"note-consolidation:claim:<1>","status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","predicate":"uses_evidence_hydration_strategy","text":"SourceHydrator should use bounded quote hydration on evidence fallback after interpreted claims retrieval.","evidence_source_ids":["chat:<2>"]},{"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<1>","status":"RESOLVED","lifecycle_state":"non_current","title":"SourceHydrator lesson: bounded quote hydration for evidence fallback","text":"In Memory MVP, SourceHydrator should fetch only a small bounded set of raw source quotes when evidence fallback is requested after interpreted claims retrieval. This keeps factual answers grounded while avoiding prompt bloat from dumping all raw sources.","evidence_source_ids":["chat:<2>"]},{"type":"episode","id":"note-consolidation:episode:<1>","lifecycle_state":"experience","text":"Prefer bounded quote hydration over full-source dumping for evidence fallback.","evidence_source_ids":["chat:<2>"]}]}
3. {"type":"episode","id":"note-consolidation:episode:<0>","score":1.604,"lifecycle_state":"experience","selection_hint":"Episode memory is reusable experience; prefer for lessons, patterns, and what-worked questions.","text":"situation=In Memory MVP retrieval planning, ambiguous recall questions can be classified as need_memory=false by ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner. action=NoMemoryVerifier runs a short model-based verification before accepting the no-memory plan. result=Reduces brittleness of substring heuristics while still catching recall intent. lesson=For ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer a cheap model-based verifier over substring matching.","evidence":[{"source_id":"chat:<1>","kind":"DERIVED_FROM_NOTE","quote":"Lesson: for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer a cheap model-based verifier over substring matching."}]}
4. {"type":"entity","id":"entity:<0>","score":2.18,"lifecycle_state":"active","selection_hint":"Entity labels are anchors only; select them only as supporting context for selected typed memory.","entity_type":"SERVICE","name":"NoMemoryVerifier","text":"Service named NoMemoryVerifier."}
5. {"type":"claim","id":"note-consolidation:claim:<1>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed fact. Prefer this for relevant factual answers over raw sources.","predicate":"uses_evidence_hydration_strategy","subject_entity_id":"entity:<1>","text":"SourceHydrator should use bounded quote hydration on evidence fallback after interpreted claims retrieval.","context":"Memory MVP factual-answer grounding behavior","confidence":0.95,"importance":8,"evidence":[{"source_id":"chat:<2>","kind":"DERIVED_FROM_NOTE","quote":"Lesson: for source evidence hydration, prefer bounded quote hydration over dumping all sources into the runtime prompt."}]}
6. {"type":"claim","id":"note-consolidation:claim:<0>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed fact. Prefer this for relevant factual answers over raw sources.","predicate":"verifies_ambiguous_no_memory_recall_with_model_call","subject_entity_id":"entity:<0>","text":"NoMemoryVerifier should use a cheap model-based verifier for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions rather than substring matching.","context":"Memory MVP retrieval planning","confidence":0.98,"importance":4,"evidence":[{"source_id":"chat:<1>","kind":"DERIVED_FROM_NOTE","quote":"Lesson: for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer a cheap model-based verifier over substring matching."}]}
7. {"type":"episode","id":"note-consolidation:episode:<1>","score":1.0,"lifecycle_state":"experience","selection_hint":"Episode memory is reusable experience; prefer for lessons, patterns, and what-worked questions.","text":"situation=In Memory MVP, factual answers may need raw source evidence after interpreted claims retrieval. action=Implemented SourceHydrator strategy to hydrate only a small bounded set of raw source quotes when evidence fallback is requested. result=Answers remain grounded in source evidence while avoiding runtime prompt bloat from dumping all raw sources. lesson=Prefer bounded quote hydration over full-source dumping for evidence fallback.","evidence":[{"source_id":"chat:<2>","kind":"DERIVED_FROM_NOTE","quote":"Lesson: for source evidence hydration, prefer bounded quote hydration over dumping all sources into the runtime prompt."}]}

        Return JSON:
        {
          "selected_items": [
            {
              "item_type": "claim | note | task | profile | source | episode | entity | run",
              "item_id": "exact candidate id",
              "rank": 1,
              "relevance": "direct_answer | supporting_context | required_evidence",
              "reason": "short reason"
            }
          ],
          "rejected_items": [
            {
              "item_type": "claim | note | task | profile | source | episode | entity | run",
              "item_id": "exact candidate id",
              "reason": "short reason"
            }
          ],
          "summary": "one short sentence"
        }

responseFormat.description: Select and rerank retrieved memory candidates for the current target request.
responseFormat.name: memory_read_selector

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

structured.fullText: {"rejected_items":[{"item_id":"chat:<1>","item_type":"source","reason":"Source evidence not needed because episode already provides the lesson."},{"item_id":"chat:<2>","item_type":"source","reason":"Different component (SourceHydrator), not relevant to NoMemoryVerifier lesson."},{"item_id":"entity:<0>","item_type":"entity","reason":"Entity anchor unnecessary since selected episode is self-contained."},{"item_id":"note-consolidation:claim:<1>","item_type":"claim","reason":"Different topic/component (SourceHydrator)."},{"item_id":"note-consolidation:claim:<0>","item_type":"claim","reason":"Relevant but redundant; episode is preferred for reusable lesson questions."},{"item_id":"note-consolidation:episode:<1>","item_type":"episode","reason":"Different lesson about SourceHydrator evidence hydration."}],"selected_items":[{"item_id":"note-consolidation:episode:<0>","item_type":"episode","rank":1,"reason":"Direct reusable lesson for NoMemoryVerifier on ambiguous no-memory recall decisions.","relevance":"direct_answer"}],"summary":"Selected the NoMemoryVerifier episode as the minimal and best-fit reusable lesson memory."}
